title: Its A Mad Mad Mad Mad Marge

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer gets home from work, presumably crashes his car or has some mishap
jokes.punchline: His signature catchphrase and scream indicating another domestic disaster
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - high character integration and quotability but predictable

jokes.text: Now be careful with those video cameras, children. In order to buy them, the school board had to eliminate geography. This globe will never spin again.
jokes.setup: Teacher warning kids to be careful with expensive video cameras
jokes.punchline: Reveals the absurd trade-off of eliminating geography and the melodramatic globe line
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure with the melodramatic 'never spin again' payoff. Classic Simpsons satire of education budget priorities.

jokes.text: Now, class, I want you to be creative with your video projects. I don't want to see 30 Blair Witch knockoffs.
jokes.setup: Teacher asking for creativity in video projects
jokes.punchline: Reference to Blair Witch Project trend and predictable student behavior
jokes.explanation: Timely reference but relies on pop culture name-drop without much twist.

jokes.text: Stop Hitting Yourself, take one.
jokes.setup: Kids making video projects
jokes.punchline: Bart treating annoying sibling behavior as a formal film production
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart characterization - turning childish behavior into art. Great economy.

jokes.text: Tonight on the Discovery Channel, Inside Lisa's Nose. What will we find, boogers or Nazi gold?
jokes.setup: Bart narrating a documentary-style investigation of Lisa's nose
jokes.punchline: Escalates from expected 'boogers' to absurd 'Nazi gold'
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons absurdist escalation. The Nazi gold twist is completely unexpected yet somehow logical in Bart's world.

jokes.text: Otto, a red traffic light means what? Oooh. No time for brainteasers. Today's the day I ask my girlfriend to take a ride on the matrimony pony.
jokes.setup: Teacher asking Otto a basic traffic question
jokes.punchline: Otto dismisses basic knowledge as 'brainteasers' and uses euphemistic 'matrimony pony'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Otto characterization - avoiding responsibility while using hilariously awkward romantic language.

jokes.text: We met in the summer of love. Woodstock, '99.
jokes.setup: Otto referencing the romantic 'summer of love'
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was the commercialized, chaotic Woodstock '99
jokes.explanation: Sharp contrast between the idealistic '60s reference and the notorious '99 event. Perfect Otto delusion.

jokes.text: Quick! I need some water! Eight dollars! Not in this lifetime.
jokes.setup: Otto desperately needing water at Woodstock
jokes.punchline: Outrageous $8 price and Otto's refusal despite desperation
jokes.explanation: Solid commentary on concert price gouging, but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Oh, Otto. Of course my answer is- Oooh, wait, wait, wait, wait. This solo is kick-ass!
jokes.setup: Becky about to accept Otto's marriage proposal
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by guitar solo mid-acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that establishes Becky's character and the music conflict to come.

jokes.text: Ooh, and such delicate tissue paper. Huh? Zig-Zag?
jokes.setup: Marge admiring wedding invitation's delicate paper
jokes.punchline: Homer recognizes it as rolling paper brand
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Marge's innocent admiration and Homer's street knowledge.

jokes.text: Ohhh, we're 'cordially invited this Saturday... to join Otto and Becky at 742 Evergreen Terrace'? That's our address!
jokes.setup: Reading wedding invitation address
jokes.punchline: Realizes it's their own house
jokes.explanation: Functional setup for the plot but predictable reveal.

jokes.text: Hey, it'll be easy. You still got most of the stuff from Apu's wedding back there.
jokes.setup: Homer worried about wedding costs and work
jokes.punchline: Bart casually references leftover decorations from previous episode
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows the family's history of hosting events and Bart's practical thinking.

jokes.text: Visual gag: Elephant trumpeting in background
jokes.setup: Mention of leftover wedding stuff
jokes.punchline: Actual elephant from previous wedding still there
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons visual callback - the absurd made mundane.

jokes.text: Oh, Mom, you're not supposed to throw rice anymore. Birds eat it, their stomachs swell, and they explode. Why am I just learning this now?
jokes.setup: Marge planning to use rice at wedding
jokes.punchline: Lisa's dark explanation and Marge's innocent confusion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Lisa's knowledge vs. Marge's traditional thinking.

jokes.text: Well, the important thing is you came. We're leaving.
jokes.setup: Otto trying to find positive in parents' harsh words
jokes.punchline: Parents immediately leave after brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and structure with absurd family dynamics

jokes.text: Always a bridesmaid, only occasionally a bride.
jokes.setup: Standard 'always a bridesmaid, never a bride' saying
jokes.punchline: Modified to acknowledge multiple marriages/divorces
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on familiar phrase that perfectly captures the character's romantic history.

jokes.text: So, any words for the bride and groom? Not now, Bart. I'm trying to urinate. You don't seem to be trying very hard.
jokes.setup: Bart asking for wedding interview while Homer is in bathroom
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt response and Bart's literal observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer and Bart interaction - Homer's crude honesty meets Bart's persistent journalism.

jokes.text: Dad! The bride and groom are supposed to cut the wedding cake. Oh, that's just superstition.
jokes.setup: Lisa reminding Homer of wedding tradition
jokes.punchline: Homer dismissing it while clearly just wanting to eat cake
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer selfishness disguised as wisdom. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Oh, we'll be fine. Otto's got a clean police record and he doesn't do any needle drugs.
jokes.setup: Reassuring about relationship prospects
jokes.punchline: Setting incredibly low bar for boyfriend standards
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - Becky's low standards reveal her background and naivety.

jokes.text: Well, the real key according to sexperts is mutual interests.
jokes.setup: Marge giving relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Uses term 'sexperts' innocently for relationship experts
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay with good character voice but not particularly original

jokes.text: He refers to our lovemaking as the Head bangers Ball!
jokes.setup: Discussion of relationship compatibility
jokes.punchline: Otto's crude music reference for intimate moments
jokes.explanation: Appropriately cringe for the character mismatch, though relies on sexual innuendo.

jokes.text: That's how I polished Homer into the perfect- Homer! No! That's ice!
jokes.setup: Marge explaining how she perfected Homer
jokes.punchline: Interrupted by Homer doing something stupid with ice
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing undermines Marge's claim about perfecting Homer. Good misdirection structure.

jokes.text: As for the matter of my honorarium. What? You know, my emolument. Huh? Pay me- $300.
jokes.setup: Reverend trying to get paid with fancy vocabulary
jokes.punchline: Has to dumb it down progressively to simple 'pay me'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure showing communication breakdown

jokes.text: 300? I could have gotten Rick Dees for that.
jokes.setup: Reverend asking for $300 payment
jokes.punchline: Otto comparing to washed-up radio DJ
jokes.explanation: Dated reference that relies on audience knowing Rick Dees' decline.

jokes.text: You got Poison to play at our wedding? We're Cyanide. A loving tribute to Poison. We need a ride home!
jokes.setup: Becky excited about famous band Poison
jokes.punchline: It's a tribute band named after another poison, and they need a ride
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with the poison theme carried through. The 'need a ride home' adds to their pathetic status.

jokes.text: You got Poison to play at our wedding? We're Cyanide. A loving tribute to Poison. We need a ride home!
jokes.setup: Becky thinking Otto got famous band Poison
jokes.punchline: They're tribute band Cyanide, and they need transportation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with escalating disappointment and practical concerns

jokes.text: Moe, can I give you some shrimp for the road? Nah. I'll just take the ring pillow... and these, uh, seven presents I bring.
jokes.setup: Homer offering Moe food to take home
jokes.punchline: Moe stealing wedding gifts while claiming he brought them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe characterization - turning theft into a favor. The specific count of 'seven presents' adds authenticity.

jokes.text: Yeah. It's better now than when you're too old and fat to get another man.
jokes.setup: Trying to comfort Becky about failed wedding
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest 'comfort' about aging and prospects
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Patty/Selma's brutal honesty disguised as helpfulness. Dark but character-true.

jokes.text: Remember that talk we had about you not wrecking people's weddings?
jokes.setup: Bart reminding Homer about volunteering their house
jokes.punchline: Homer's counter about Bart ruining the wedding
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic - both deflecting blame with valid points.

jokes.text: A man innocently changes his oil when... a two-ton car comes crashing down!
jokes.setup: Documentary-style narration of car accident
jokes.punchline: Dramatic presentation of obvious car maintenance danger
jokes.explanation: Decent parody of dramatic TV documentary style

jokes.text: Who'd have thought you could hold up a car with a wicker basket?
jokes.setup: Car falling on Homer while changing oil
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual observation about inadequate safety equipment
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - finding the technical problem while ignoring the danger.

jokes.text: So I usually sneak a little meat juice into her vegetables. Wow! You're a real-life Martha Stewart. I mean, without the evil.
jokes.setup: Marge describing how she tricks Lisa into eating meat
jokes.punchline: Becky's Martha Stewart comparison with 'without the evil' qualifier
jokes.explanation: Timely reference to Martha Stewart's legal troubles. Sets up Becky as seemingly innocent.

jokes.text: Mom, this gravy tastes better than God's sweat.
jokes.setup: Homer complimenting Marge's gravy
jokes.punchline: Bizarre religious comparison that's somehow a compliment
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly weird Homer compliment. The religious imagery mixed with bodily functions is perfectly absurd yet somehow sincere.

jokes.text: What are these things in the mashed potatoes? That's the skins. I left them on. Well, nobody's perfect. Let me just pick those out.
jokes.setup: Marge finding potato skins in Becky's cooking
jokes.punchline: Marge's passive-aggressive response and attempt to 'fix' them
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Marge's growing resentment. Her politeness masking criticism is very realistic.

jokes.text: What are these things in the mashed potatoes? That's the skins. I left them on. Well, nobody's perfect. Let me just pick those out.
jokes.setup: Homer questioning potato skins in mashed potatoes
jokes.punchline: Marge's passive-aggressive response to Becky's cooking innovation
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Marge's growing resentment with realistic family dynamics

jokes.text: No! Oh, Becky, your potatoes are the best I've ever had.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to remove potato skins
jokes.punchline: Homer stops her and praises Becky's cooking over Marge's
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Marge's insecurity with Homer's thoughtless preference

jokes.text: Yeah, Mom. I need something great. Milhouse has footage of himself falling down the stairs.
jokes.setup: Bart defending his dangerous video project
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's self-injury as competition standard
jokes.explanation: Good Milhouse reference and captures the kids' competitive mindset about dangerous content.

jokes.text: We're jamming, Mom. She's painting my music... and I'm playing to her painting.
jokes.setup: Marge interrupting Lisa and Becky's artistic collaboration
jokes.punchline: Lisa's pretentious description of their creative synergy
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa character moment with artistic pretension

jokes.text: Actually, you could just tie them at the ends. That way the elastic doesn't wear out. Yes. I hate when things get worn out.
jokes.setup: Becky offering helpful sock advice
jokes.punchline: Marge's barely contained irritation with passive-aggressive response
jokes.explanation: Marge's double meaning is perfectly passive-aggressive. Great tension building.

jokes.text: She's so helpful. And everyone loves her. And, well, this may sound crazy... but I think my family likes Becky more than me.
jokes.setup: Marge discussing her insecurities with Patty and Selma
jokes.punchline: Articulates her fear about being replaced in her own family
jokes.explanation: Good setup for paranoia plot with relatable insecurity

jokes.text: I wouldn't worry about that. Yeah. You're gonna be dead in a week anyway.
jokes.setup: Marge worried family likes Becky more than her
jokes.punchline: Casual prediction of Marge's murder
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection from comfort to horror. The casual delivery makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: Never let an attractive woman into your house. All they ever do is usurp your family and then kill you.
jokes.setup: Patty explaining dangers of attractive houseguests
jokes.punchline: Escalates from family disruption to murder with matter-of-fact delivery
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with perfect character voice and paranoid worldview

jokes.text: Like that documentary, The Hand that Rocks the Cradle. That was a movie.
jokes.setup: Using movie as evidence of dangerous women
jokes.punchline: Marge pointing out it's fiction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Patty/Selma's paranoid logic vs. Marge's rationality.

jokes.text: Or club you with an antique wooden doll. Oh, yeah. Yeah, that was a good one.
jokes.setup: Listing ways attractive women kill housewives
jokes.punchline: Specific weapon choice appreciated as entertainment rather than warning
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing they treat thrillers as how-to guides

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Yeah, that was a good one. Oh. But first, she's going to seduce your husband. Becky's going to seduce Homer? [Both Groan] It's an act of violence, not love.
jokes.setup: Discussing movie plot about seduction
jokes.punchline: The absurd idea of anyone wanting to seduce Homer
jokes.explanation: Great character humor - even the paranoid twins find Homer unseduceable.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. You can learn a lot from your kids if you just listen to 'em.
jokes.setup: Becky knowing Lisa's favorite food better than Marge
jokes.punchline: Condescending parenting advice to actual parent
jokes.explanation: Perfectly passive-aggressive. Becky's seemingly innocent advice is actually insulting.

jokes.text: Why do I always think of the perfect thing to say when it's too late? 'Shut up, Becky!' Oh, that would have been sweet.
jokes.setup: Marge frustrated after awkward encounter
jokes.punchline: Her 'perfect' comeback is just telling Becky to shut up
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - her idea of a devastating comeback is hilariously mild.

jokes.text: The 'brakes cut' light! Hmm?
jokes.setup: Car having brake problems
jokes.punchline: Existence of specific 'brakes cut' warning light
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons absurdity - the car anticipates sabotage with a specific warning light.

jokes.text: Wow. Tough glass. I still say ours is bigger.
jokes.setup: Marge crashes into building
jokes.punchline: Bystanders casually commenting on glass strength and size
jokes.explanation: Good background character humor - treating car crash as casual window shopping opportunity.

jokes.text: I don't know. Get some attention from a handsome police officer. That's crazy!
jokes.setup: Wiggum suggesting Marge cut her own brakes
jokes.punchline: His reasoning is to get attention from him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum logic - assuming people would endanger themselves to meet him.

jokes.text: Fine. Let me tell you what I tell everybody who comes in here. The law is powerless to help you.
jokes.setup: Marge asking for police help
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's standard response admits police incompetence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy. Wiggum's honest admission of systemic failure delivered casually.

jokes.text: Do I have to be dead before you'll help me? Well, not dead. Dying.
jokes.setup: Marge asking about police response threshold
jokes.punchline: Wiggum clarifies the minimal standard for police intervention
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with dark humor about police response standards

jokes.text: Just show me the knife, in your back, not too deep... but it should be able to stand by itself.
jokes.setup: Marge asking what it takes to get help
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's specific requirements for stabbing evidence
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd police procedure. The specific visual requirement makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: And God said, Gather ye two of every flavor. Anoint them with 62 sauces, whipped cream and nuts. And ye shall call it... the Ark!
jokes.setup: Describing elaborate ice cream sundae
jokes.punchline: Biblical parody with Noah's Ark reference
jokes.explanation: Clever biblical parody that works with ice cream context. Good wordplay structure.

jokes.text: Don't mess with me! I've got jimmies! I can only see a horrible rainbow!
jokes.setup: Marge throwing ice cream toppings
jokes.punchline: Threatening with sprinkles and colorful chaos
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between innocent ice cream toppings and violent threat. 'Horrible rainbow' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Usurper! Usurper! Usurper!
jokes.setup: Marge's paranoia reaching breaking point
jokes.punchline: Screams formal accusation while attacking with ice cream
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to full paranoid break with formal vocabulary

jokes.text: Shut up, Becky! There, I finally said it.
jokes.setup: Marge's earlier regret about not saying this
jokes.punchline: Finally delivers her 'perfect' comeback in completely wrong context
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback structure. Marge's satisfaction at terrible timing is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I can only see a horrible rainbow!
jokes.setup: Ice cream fight chaos in parlor
jokes.punchline: Describes colorful ice cream mess as horrible rainbow
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist description of chaotic colorful scene

jokes.text: Drop the cone, sugar! Whoopsie. Yeah, powerless to help you, not punish you.
jokes.setup: Wiggum arresting Marge after claiming police were powerless
jokes.punchline: Clarifies distinction between helping and punishing citizens
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that perfectly explains police priorities and incompetence

jokes.text: Yeah, powerless to help you, not punish you.
jokes.setup: Marge pointing out Wiggum said law was powerless
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's distinction between helping and punishing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that reveals the system's priorities. Perfect Wiggum logic.

jokes.text: This isn't over, Becky! I know where you live! My house!
jokes.setup: Marge making dramatic threat to Becky
jokes.punchline: Realizes the threat makes no sense since Becky lives with her
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of dramatic threat. Marge's realization makes the threat absurd.

jokes.text: It was healthy. He reciprocated. He reciprocated!
jokes.setup: Judge asking about Marge's Ringo Starr fixation
jokes.punchline: Marge's defensive response suggesting relationship was mutual
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier episode. Marge's defensive repetition suggests delusion.

jokes.text: I see. And this 'god,' is he in this room right now? Oh, yes. He's kind of everywhere.
jokes.setup: Judge treating Marge's prayer as delusion
jokes.punchline: Marge's innocent theological response sounds like crazy talk
jokes.explanation: Good setup showing how normal religious belief can sound delusional in legal context.

jokes.text: Marge Simpson, you give us no choice but to declare you utterly- I'm not insane! You didn't let me finish. Insane!
jokes.setup: Judge about to deliver verdict
jokes.punchline: Marge's interruption proves the verdict before it's delivered
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection structure. Marge's outburst ironically confirms the verdict.

jokes.text: Run, Marge! Run! Pump those crazy legs!
jokes.setup: Marge escaping from courthouse
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiastic but unhelpful encouragement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - supportive but completely unhelpful. 'Crazy legs' adds insult to injury.

jokes.text: Even here at Juggernauts where it's wet T-shirt month! But for now... this reporter prays that Mad Marge can be captured before she kills again. Kills again? She hasn't killed once!
jokes.setup: Sensationalized news report about Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer pointing out the logical flaw in 'kills again'
jokes.explanation: Good media satire about sensationalized reporting. Homer's logical observation is surprisingly astute.

jokes.text: Kills again? She hasn't killed once! Shouldn't we be out there trying to find her?
jokes.setup: News report claiming Marge will 'kill again'
jokes.punchline: Lisa points out logical flaw while Homer suggests actually helping
jokes.explanation: Good logical observation with character-appropriate responses

jokes.text: Freeze, you crazy mommy! That a boy. But you gotta aim a little higher. There you go. That's a kill shot.
jokes.setup: Wiggum training Ralph to catch Marge
jokes.punchline: Teaching child to make 'kill shots' at escaped mother
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about police training combined with awful parenting. Perfect Wiggum incompetence.

jokes.text: who's your favorite Native American warrior? Crazy Horse! Whoo, whoo, whoo!
jokes.setup: Krusty doing insensitive comedy bit about 'crazy'
jokes.punchline: Marge playing along with offensive stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Dated and problematic humor that relies on offensive stereotypes. Even for its time, this feels lazy.

jokes.text: Hmm! I really should read the whole headline before I react.
jokes.setup: Marge presumably reacting to partial headline
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission about jumping to conclusions
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment for Marge. Relatable human behavior turned into character insight.

jokes.text: I mean, I am talking to myself. You are? Oh, I thought I'd made a friend.
jokes.setup: Marge questioning her sanity for talking to herself
jokes.punchline: Responds to herself as if it's another person
jokes.explanation: Clever structure showing Marge's loneliness and confusion. The innocent response makes it endearing.

jokes.text: Oh, about that. When I changed your oil... I may have drained your brake fluid. I didn't say anything 'cause I thought you'd be mad. Oh, boy. My bad.
jokes.setup: Marge confronting Becky about brake sabotage
jokes.punchline: Becky reveals it was innocent incompetence with casual 'my bad' response
jokes.explanation: Good resolution subverting conspiracy with simple incompetence

jokes.text: Oh, about that. When I changed your oil... I may have drained your brake fluid. I didn't say anything 'cause I thought you'd be mad. Oh, boy. My bad.
jokes.setup: Marge accusing Becky of cutting brakes
jokes.punchline: Innocent explanation reveals dangerous incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good resolution that makes Becky sympathetic while maintaining the danger. 'My bad' is perfectly casual.

jokes.text: Hey, no biggie. I was trying to steal your family. I even thought of a good place to bury you. Then I didn't have a shovel, so I went to the hardware store. They have six different kinds. I was, like, 'Later'
jokes.setup: Becky casually admitting to family theft attempt
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by shovel variety instead of completing murder plan
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy escalation. The ADD-like distraction from murder planning is perfectly absurd and character-specific.

jokes.text: We put enough tranquilizers in there to take down Jonathan Winters. Oh, I've got too much to do to take a nap right now.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining massive tranquilizer dose
jokes.punchline: Marge treating it as inconvenient nap timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge response - treating medical emergency as scheduling conflict. Jonathan Winters reference is nicely specific.

jokes.text: And, Homer, since you're dressed for it... I got some S & M for ya- scrubbing and mopping.
jokes.setup: Marge addressing Homer in his outfit
jokes.punchline: S&M revealed as cleaning tasks, not sexual reference
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection pun that fits Marge's character perfectly. Good structure with the pause building anticipation.
